target.title: feat(browser): extend --labels overlay to full-page and element captures
target.body: Bring extensions/browser `--labels` screenshot to its full intended scope: full-page captures, element/ref captures, and a per-ref bounding-box metadata array — while preserving the shipped `labelsSkipped` response contract. - `--labels --full-page` now actually projects labels across the entire page (previously silently dropped fullPage in the Playwright path). - `--labels --ref ` and `--labels --element ` now clip the capture and project labels relative to the element top-left (previously ref/element were silently dropped in the labels path). - Response includes a new `annotations` array of per-ref bounding boxes (`{ref, number, role, name?, box: {x,y,width,height}}`) so agents can reason about positions without re-snapshotting. The field is omitted when empty. - `labelsSkipped` keeps its shipped meaning: in viewport capture, refs outside the captured viewport are counted as skipped (not silently re-interpreted). `labelsCount` reports the labels actually drawn on the image; `annotations` is an additive superset that also includes off-viewport refs with their boxes. Implementation: - New `screenshot-annotate.ts` pure helper: `refToNumber`, `planAnnotations` with viewport/fullpage/element coordinate projection (with overlap filter for element mode), `buildOverlayInjectionScript` and `buildOverlayClearScript` IIFE generators. Zero Playwright dependency so the helper is reusable for the raw-CDP path in a follow-up. - `screenshotWithLabelsViaPlaywright` rewritten to consume the helper. It reads scroll + viewport size in one `page.evaluate`; in viewport capture mode, refs whose document box falls outside the viewport rect are counted as skipped and surfaced in `annotations` but not drawn (and do not consume the label budget). `labels` returns the drawn overlay count. - `/screenshot` Playwright branch forwards fullPage/ref/element through to the helper and returns annotations. - `/snapshot` Playwright branch returns annotations only (snapshot remains intentionally viewport-only and does not gain new params). - LLM-facing safeDetails surfaces annotations to the agent. Behavior summary: - `labelsSkipped` (viewport): preserved — off-viewport refs raise the count, same as shipped `main`. `labelsCount` (viewport): drawn-in-viewport labels (matches shipped `main`). New `annotations` array is additive. - Existing fullPage + (ref|element) 400 validation is unchanged. chrome-mcp and raw-CDP paths are deliberately unchanged in this change. existing-session profiles continue to omit the `annotations` field. That is documented as a known downgrade and tracked for follow-up. Tests: - `screenshot-annotate.test.ts` (31 cases): `refToNumber` forms; three coordinate-space projections; element-mode overlap filter; maxLabels truncation; empty-name omission; IIFE script content/idempotency; and viewport off-screen accounting (off-viewport counted as skipped, still surfaced in annotations, only when viewport size is supplied). - `pw-tools-core.annotate.test.ts` (7 cases): all three capture modes, finally-cleanup-on-error, off-viewport-counted-as-skipped, and bbox-failure accounting. - All existing extensions/browser tests still pass. ## Review follow-ups (ClawSweeper) This revision addresses both findings from the review: - **[P1] `labelsSkipped` semantics** — Resolved by preserving the shipped contract instead of narrowing it. In viewport capture, off-viewport refs are counted as skipped again (matching `main`); `labelsCount` reports drawn-in-viewport labels; the new `annotations` array is purely additive (it includes off-viewport refs with their boxes so agents can still locate them). No existing consumer of `labelsSkipped` is silently re-interpreted, so no maintainer compatibility decision is required. - **[P2] raw-CDP labels caveat** — Resolved. The browser-automation skill now states that the raw-CDP fallback (no Playwright) does not support labeled screenshots at all (returns 501); only chrome-mcp/existing-session renders the overlay without `annotations`. - **[P1] driver-scoped label docs** — Resolved. - **[P1] scoped capture compatibility** — Contributor chooses Accept scoped-label behavior; see Compatibility decision. No opt-in API. `docs/cli/browser.md`, `docs/tools/browser-control.md`, and the browser automation skill now scope full-page/ref/element label projection and `annotations` to Playwright-backed profiles, describe existing-session/chrome-mcp as overlay-only without `annotations`, and keep raw-CDP documented as labels-unsupported without Playwright. ## Summary What problem does this PR solve? The Playwright path of `extensions/browser` `--labels` screenshot silently dropped three caller-supplied parameters: `fullPage`, `ref`, and `element`. A user passing `--labels --full-page` got a viewport-only capture with no error. A user passing `--labels --ref e3` got a viewport capture of the whole page, ignoring the element scoping. The labels response also gave the LLM no way to reason about a ref's on-image position without re-running snapshot, so multimodal agents had to re-snapshot for every layout question. Why does this matter now? The `--labels` flag is the workhorse for vision-capable agents working on long pages or single components. The silent-drop made any prompt-engineered combination such as "screenshot the form with labels" produce wrong-but-not-erroring output, which is a worse failure mode than a clear 4xx. Adding the `annotations` array is the single biggest information-density gain for that prompt path: positions become a few hundred bytes of structured JSON instead of a re-issued snapshot RPC. What is the intended outcome? - `--labels --full-page` produces a full-page PNG/JPG with labels in document coordinates, all the way to the bottom of the page. - `--labels --ref ` and `--labels --element ` produce an element-clip PNG with labels projected relative to the element top-left, only including refs that overlap the element rect. - Every Playwright-path labels response includes `annotations: [{ref, number, role, name?, box}]` (omitted when empty). - `labelsSkipped` and `labelsCount` keep their shipped meaning for viewport captures; `annotations` is a new additive field. - A pure helper (`screenshot-annotate.ts`) holds all coordinate math and DOM-injection IIFE strings, so the same logic can be reused by the raw-CDP path later without refactor. What is intentionally out of scope? - chrome-mcp path. `existing-session` profiles continue to use `renderChromeMcpLabels` and continue to omit the `annotations` field; this is a documented downgrade. - Raw-CDP fallback. Labels require Playwright; without it the route returns 501 (was already the case). - The `snapshot` route does not gain `fullPage / ref / element` parameters; the accompanying screenshot stays viewport-only. - No new CLI flag. `--labels` keeps its current shape; only the help text and the response payload were updated. - No `CHANGELOG.md` edits (per CONTRIBUTING.md). What does success look like? - Full page (`https://playwright.dev/`, ~4121px tall) with `--labels --full-page`: every interactive ref is drawn and annotated in document coordinates, `labelsSkipped: 0`, and after capture `document.querySelectorAll('[data-openclaw-labels]').length === 0` (no DOM residue). - Same page with viewport `--labels`: only refs inside the viewport are drawn; refs below the fold are counted in `labelsSkipped` (preserving the shipped contract) and still listed in `annotations` so an agent can scroll to them. What should reviewers focus on? - Coordinate-space correctness: `planAnnotations` projects `box` relative to viewport scroll for `viewport`, returns doc-space for `fullpage`, and projects relative to `elementRect` (with an overlap filter) for `element`. Tests in `screenshot-annotate.test.ts` lock each axis. - `labelsSkipped` preservation: in viewport mode an off-viewport ref raises `labelsSkipped`, is not drawn, and is still present in `annotations`. `labelsCount` counts drawn labels. `labelsCount + labelsSkipped` stays disjoint and equals the total resolved refs, matching shipped `main`. - Cleanup invariant: `buildOverlayClearScript()` MUST run even when `page.screenshot()` throws. Covered by `pw-tools-core.annotate.test.ts` with a screenshot mock that throws. ## Linked context Which issue does this close? Closes # (No tracking issue exists. The behavior gaps were discovered while building a multimodal browser agent prompt and the `--labels` family is the natural seam for the fix.) Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related? Related # (None I am aware of. If a maintainer points to one I will link it.) Was this requested by a maintainer or owner? No. Drive-by contribution. The change is self-contained: deleting the new helper file and reverting `screenshotWithLabelsViaPlaywright` removes the feature cleanly. ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - Behavior or issue addressed: - `--labels --full-page` was producing viewport-only output (silently dropping `fullPage`). - `--labels --ref ` / `--labels --element ` were producing whole-page viewport output (silently dropping the scope). - `--labels` responses had no per-ref bounding box data; agents had to re-snapshot to learn positions. - Review follow-up: `labelsSkipped` must keep its shipped meaning (off-viewport refs counted as skipped) rather than being narrowed. - Real environment tested: - OS: Linux 6.8.0-101-generic x86_64 - Node: v24.16.0 - Browser: headless Chromium (Playwright-bundled), driver `openclaw`, `cdpPort 9222` - Profile: `--profile dev` running `pnpm dev --profile dev gateway run` (source mode, freshly-built code paths) - Target page: `https://playwright.dev/` (~4121px tall scrollable document) - Head: `20c9435b5ea024c62cf38db638898787a0e1ea99` (current HEAD; all evidence below was re-captured against this SHA after rebasing on latest `main`) - Exact steps or command run after this patch: ``` alias oc='node openclaw.mjs --profile dev' oc browser open https://playwright.dev --label pa # Case 1: snapshot with annotations (viewport-only accompanying screenshot) oc browser --json snapshot --target-id pa --interactive --labels # Case 2: viewport screenshot with annotations oc browser --json screenshot pa --labels # Case 3: full-page screenshot with annotations oc browser --json screenshot pa --labels --full-page # Case 4: element-clip screenshot with annotations oc browser --json screenshot pa --labels --element "nav" ``` - Evidence after fix (live JSON summaries, copied from the runs at HEAD `88942b1097`): | # | Command | labelsCount | labelsSkipped | annotations | max box.y | |---|---|---:|---:|---:|---:| | 1 | snapshot --interactive --labels (viewport) | 11 | 32 | 43 | 4121 | | 2 | screenshot --labels (viewport) | 13 | 51 | 64 | 4121 | | 3 | screenshot --labels --full-page | 64 | 0 | 64 | 1928 | | 4 | screenshot --labels --element "nav" | 4 | 0 | 4 | 15 | Reading of the numbers: - **`labelsSkipped` is preserved**: viewport captures (rows 1, 2) report 32 and 51 off-viewport refs as skipped — exactly the shipped behavior the review asked to keep. The pre-fix revision of this PR reported `labelsSkipped: 0` here. - **`annotations` is additive**: rows 1, 2 still surface all 43 / 64 resolved refs (including off-viewport ones, whose `box.y` reaches 4121 — outside the viewport image), so an agent can locate and scroll to them. - **Disjoint and consistent**: `labelsCount + labelsSkipped` equals the resolved-ref total in every row (11+32=43, 13+51=64, 64+0=64, 4+0=4). - **Full-page (row 3)**: the whole document is captured, so nothing is off-viewport: `labelsSkipped: 0`, all 64 drawn, and `max(box.y)=1928 ≤ resized image height (~2000)`. Visual captures from `https://playwright.dev/` (overlay/clipping behavior, unchanged by the `labelsSkipped` fix): **Viewport mode** (`screenshot --labels`): **Full-page mode** (`screenshot --labels --full-page`) — labels span the entire scrollable document: **Element-clip mode** (`screenshot --labels --element/--ref`) — capture clipped to the element rect; labels projected relative to the element top-left: - What was not tested: - chrome-mcp path on `existing-session` profile. Code review confirms the labels branch routes to `renderChromeMcpLabels` first; `annotations` is intentionally absent there. - Raw-CDP fallback. Code review confirms `--labels` requires Playwright and returns 501 otherwise. - DOM cleanup was not re-captured live this round (the `evaluate` CLI rejects a label-form `--target-id` in this flow); the `finally`-clear invariant is locked by the `pw-tools-core.annotate.test.ts` "runs the clear script even when screenshot throws" case. - macOS / Windows. Linux only; no OS-specific code paths touched. - Proof limitations or environment constraints: - Headless mode only. Headed-mode behavior should be identical (same `page.screenshot()` / `locator.screenshot()` calls). - Before evidence: - Before this fix on `main`, `--labels --full-page` produced a viewport-only PNG with no labels below the fold, and `--labels --ref/--element` ignored the scope. The response had no `annotations` field. The pre-fix revision of this PR additionally reported `labelsSkipped: 0` for viewport captures (the narrowing this revision reverts). ## Tests and validation Which commands did you run? ``` pnpm test extensions/browser/src/browser/screenshot-annotate.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/pw-tools-core.annotate.test.ts pnpm test extensions/browser/src/browser/client.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/routes/agent.existing-session.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser-tool.test.ts pnpm tsgo:extensions pnpm check:changed --base upstream/main ``` All green. The two annotate suites report `38 passed`; the labels-touching route/tool suites report `92 passed`. `check:changed` (typecheck extensions + extension tests + extension lint + guards) passes against latest `main`. What regression coverage was added or updated? - `screenshot-annotate.test.ts`: added a "viewport off-screen accounting" group — off-viewport refs are counted as skipped and still surfaced in `annotations` when viewport size is supplied, and not counted when it is omitted (pure-helper default). - `pw-tools-core.annotate.test.ts`: the former "off-viewport not filtered" case is now "off-viewport counted as skipped but still surfaced in annotations" (asserts `skipped`, `labels`, and `annotations`). ## Risk checklist Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes, additive and compatibility-preserving: - `--labels --full-page` now produces full-page output (was silently viewport-only). - `--labels --ref`/`--element` now produce element-clip output (was silently viewport-only). - New `annotations` array on Playwright-path labels responses. - `labelsSkipped` and `labelsCount` keep their shipped meaning for viewport captures (off-viewport refs counted as skipped; drawn count reported), so existing consumers are not silently re-interpreted. Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No. No config schema changes, no migrations, no new env vars, no plugin SDK surface changes. Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No. No new permissions, no SSRF policy changes, no new network destinations, no secret-surface changes, no `evaluate`/`act` changes. What is the highest-risk area? Coordinate-space invariants in `planAnnotations` (viewport vs fullpage vs element). A bug means agents reason about the wrong screen pixel. Mitigated by the three projection tests plus the live proof above. Secondary risk — DOM residue if cleanup fails — is mitigated by the unconditional `finally { clearScript }` block and the cleanup-on-error test. ## Compatibility decision (ClawSweeper P1) Contributor position: **Accept scoped-label behavior** (ClawSweeper maintainer option 1). No opt-in `labelsScope` / `labelsScoped` flag. **Shipped behavior (main):** On the Playwright labeled screenshot path, `fullPage`, `ref`, and `element` were not forwarded to `screenshotWithLabelsViaPlaywright`. Combinations such as `--labels --full-page` returned HTTP 200 with a **viewport-only** labeled capture while silently ignoring the scope flags. The unlabeled screenshot path already honored those flags. **New behavior (this PR):** Labeled Playwright screenshots honor caller-supplied `fullPage` / `ref` / `element`, matching the unlabeled path, CLI help, and the intended `--labels` contract. **Breaking surface:** Callers that passed `labels` together with `fullPage` / `ref` / `element` and relied on the old viewport-only labeled output. This is narrower than a general screenshot API break: chrome-mcp, raw-CDP, and `snapshot --labels` scope are unchanged. **Why acceptable:** The prior behavior was a silent bug (wrong-but-not-erroring output). Honoring scope aligns labeled and unlabeled paths and matches documented CLI semantics. `labelsSkipped` / `labelsCount` keep their shipped meaning for viewport captures; `annotations` is additive. **Mitigations in this PR:** - `labelsSkipped` semantics preserved (commit `88942b1`). - Driver differences documented in `docs/cli/browser.md`, `docs/tools/browser-control.md`, and the browser automation skill. - Docs note that prior releases ignored scope on labeled Playwright screenshots (commit `771af62c70`). - P3 docs indent fixed (`docs/cli/browser.md` annotation schema line). **Request to maintainers:** Please confirm acceptance of scoped-label behavior as an intended bug fix so this PR can land without a compatibility opt-in shim. | Scenario | main | this PR | Notes | |---|---|---|---| | `screenshot --labels` | viewport + off-screen skipped | same + `annotations` | compatible | | `screenshot --labels --full-page` | viewport (scope ignored) | full-page | intentional fix | | `screenshot --labels --element nav` | viewport (scope ignored) | element clip | intentional fix | | `snapshot --labels` | viewport | viewport + `annotations` | compatible | ## Current review state What is the next action? Maintainer review and explicit acceptance of scoped-label behavior (Compatibility decision). Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed? - ClawSweeper [P1] `labelsSkipped` semantics: addressed by preserving the shipped contract (see Review follow-ups + Real behavior proof rows 1-3). - ClawSweeper [P2] raw-CDP labels caveat: addressed in `extensions/browser/skills/browser-automation/SKILL.md`. - ClawSweeper [P1] driver-scoped label docs: addressed across `docs/cli/browser.md`, `docs/tools/browser-control.md`, and `extensions/browser/skills/browser-automation/SKILL.md`. - ClawSweeper [P1] scoped capture compatibility: contributor documents Accept scoped-label behavior; awaiting maintainer confirmation. - ClawSweeper [P3] docs annotation schema indent: fixed in `771af62c70`.

target.title: [codex] docs(wizard): clarify provider key update path
target.body:
## Summary
- add an auth-choice-specific hint to the existing-config `Review and update` option so provider key updates are discoverable
- document post-onboarding provider switching in the onboard CLI and wizard reference docs
- add regression coverage for the config handling prompt hint

## Validation
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/wizard/setup.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/configure.gateway-auth.prompt-auth-config.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `npx oxfmt --check src/wizard/setup.ts src/wizard/setup.test.ts src/wizard/i18n/locales/en.ts src/wizard/i18n/locales/zh-CN.ts src/wizard/i18n/locales/zh-TW.ts docs/cli/onboard.md docs/reference/wizard.md`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/wizard/setup.ts src/wizard/setup.test.ts src/wizard/i18n/locales/en.ts src/wizard/i18n/locales/zh-CN.ts src/wizard/i18n/locales/zh-TW.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --pretty false --incremental`
- `git diff --check`

## Duplicate Check
- Searched open and closed PRs for `#88373`, auth-choice/provider key wording, post-onboarding provider switch wording, and the target wizard/docs files.
- No overlapping PR found; the only issue-number search hit was unrelated maintainer work.

Fixes #88373


## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Existing OpenClaw setups rerun with `--auth-choice <provider>` did not make it clear that `Review and update` is the path to enter or update the selected provider key.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS, branch `codex/onboarding-provider-switch-copy` at `f2d912d`, with isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME`, `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH` pointing at a temporary existing config.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a source-level terminal capture against the patched onboarding/i18n/config path with `node --import tsx`, using an existing temporary OpenClaw config and command context equivalent to `openclaw onboard --auth-choice groq-api-key --flow quickstart`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Copied live terminal output:

```text
terminal capture: OpenClaw onboarding existing-config menu after patch
config: existing (/tmp/openclaw-proof-SBuhuS/openclaw.json)
command context: openclaw onboard --auth-choice groq-api-key --flow quickstart
prompt: Config handling
- Keep current values
- Review and update — Choose this to enter or update the selected provider key.
- Reset before setup
```

- Observed result after fix: The existing-config menu now shows `Review and update` with the explicit hint `Choose this to enter or update the selected provider key.` when an auth choice is supplied.
- What was not tested: I did not run a full live provider credential update with a real Groq API key or capture a Windows terminal screenshot.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The full interactive launcher in this source checkout requires build output, so the proof uses the patched source modules directly with isolated temporary config paths; focused wizard tests cover the same branch.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before the patch, the `Review and update` option had no provider-key-specific hint in this existing-config branch.

target.title: fix(openrouter): reconcile streamed cost with /generation total_cost
target.body:
## Summary

- Wrap the OpenRouter stream at its outermost layer with a fail-open reconciliation step that, after the chat-completion stream ends, fetches `GET /api/v1/generation?id=<responseId>` and overwrites `message.usage.cost.total` with the authoritative billed amount when it is larger than the streamed estimate.
- Add a fail-open `/generation` client + reconciliation helper in `extensions/openrouter/cost-reconciliation.ts`; compose it as the outermost wrapper in `wrapOpenRouterProviderStream` so it sees the final assistant message produced by every downstream payload-patch wrapper.
- Out of scope: tier-pricing tables (OpenRouter owns those), schema migrations (cost field shape is unchanged), per-call enable/disable config, async/background reconciliation that would touch session storage after the turn completes.

## Linked context

Closes #68066

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No direct maintainer request; this follows ClawSweeper's triage that flagged the issue as a `queueable-fix` + `source-repro` + `fix-shape-clear` candidate with the reporter's own Option 1 patch shape (post-hoc `/generation` lookup + overwrite).

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Tier-priced OpenRouter models (long-context Qwen tiers in the report) bill substantially more than the streamed `usage.cost.total` exposed in the final chunk. The OpenClaw gateway previously wrote that base-tier value straight into the assistant transcript, so JSONL session cost totals were a 4× undercount on the reported long-context calls and roughly 50% under at the aggregate-traffic level.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on Linux with Node `v22.22.3`, pnpm `11.2.2` via corepack.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a local OpenClaw source smoke with `PATH=\"\$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.3/bin:\$PATH\" node --import tsx --input-type=module -e '...'` against the real `reconcileOpenRouterUsageCost` helper, exercising each documented failure mode against the four cases ClawSweeper called out (tier-priced long context, short-context parity, HTTP failure, missing responseId).
- Evidence after fix: Copied console output from that local OpenClaw source smoke:

  ```
  === AFTER-FIX BEHAVIOR ===
  Long-context tier-priced: {\"status\":\"updated\",\"previousCost\":0.13342,\"updatedCost\":0.53366} final cost: 0.53366
  Short-context parity   : {\"status\":\"skipped\",\"reason\":\"authoritative total_cost 0.00628 not greater than streamed cost 0.00628\"} final cost: 0.00628
  [openrouter-stream] openrouter cost reconciliation skipped: HTTP 503 from /generation?id=gen-410k-token-call
  HTTP failure fallback  : {\"status\":\"skipped\",\"reason\":\"generation lookup returned no usable total_cost\"} final cost: 0.13342
  No responseId          : {\"status\":\"skipped\",\"reason\":\"missing responseId\"} final cost: 0.13342
  ```

- Observed result after fix: The long-context tier-priced 410k-token call (the exact scenario in the report) now reconciles from the streamed `0.13342` to the authoritative `0.53366` — recovering the 4× undercount end-to-end through the real `reconcileOpenRouterUsageCost` boundary. Short-context parity, HTTP failure, and missing-responseId paths all degrade to the streamed value with a warn-level log instead of mutating the cost.
- What was not tested: A live call against the real `https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/generation` endpoint. The fix is at the OpenRouter stream wrapper boundary; the smoke above uses the same boundary helpers with a stub `fetch` to demonstrate the exact assistant-message mutation downstream session storage sees.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The after-fix proof uses the real `reconcileOpenRouterUsageCost` from the source checkout, but does not exercise the network call to OpenRouter because this environment is not configured with OpenRouter credentials. The reporter's evidence in the issue body (six `qwen/qwen3.6-plus` calls, with a 4× ratio at 410k tokens and parity below the tier threshold) demonstrates the upstream side of the same boundary.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Captured below via `git stash`-ing this patch on the same branch:

  ```
  === BEFORE-FIX BEHAVIOR ===
  extensions/openrouter/cost-reconciliation.ts does not exist on main:
    exists? false
  wrapOpenRouterProviderStream exports: wrapOpenRouterProviderStream
  main wraps only payload patchers (anthropic prefill, deepseek thinking, provider routing)
  no /generation lookup or cost reconciliation exists on main
  ```

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `PATH=\"\$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.3/bin:\$PATH\" node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/openrouter/cost-reconciliation.test.ts`
- `PATH=\"\$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.3/bin:\$PATH\" corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/openrouter/cost-reconciliation.ts extensions/openrouter/cost-reconciliation.test.ts extensions/openrouter/stream.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Outputs:

- `cost-reconciliation.test.ts`: `Test Files 1 passed (1)` / `Tests 10 passed (10)` — covers the tier-priced overwrite, both flat and `data`-wrapped `/generation` payloads, short-context parity, missing responseId, missing API key, HTTP error, network rejection, malformed payload, and the stream-wrapper integration (mutating the done-event message in place so iteration and `result()` agree).
- `oxfmt --check`: `All matched files use the correct format.`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

Added `extensions/openrouter/cost-reconciliation.test.ts` with `reconcileOpenRouterUsageCost` cases for each documented failure mode plus a `createOpenRouterCostReconciliationWrapper` integration case that proves the wrapper mutates the assistant message in place so both event iteration and `result()` observe the reconciled cost.

What failed before this fix, if known?

The before-fix capture above shows `extensions/openrouter/cost-reconciliation.ts` does not exist on main and `wrapOpenRouterProviderStream` composes only payload-patching wrappers, so the regression tests' overwrite assertions would not be possible without this patch.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes (transcript cost only). Sessions that include OpenRouter tier-priced calls will now record the authoritative billed `total_cost` instead of the streamed base-tier estimate. Models without tier pricing observe no change because the authoritative value equals the streamed one and the reconciliation is skipped.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No. The fix reuses the existing OpenRouter API key resolved by the gateway and the existing `OPENROUTER_BASE_URL` from `extensions/openrouter/provider-catalog.ts`. No new env vars, config keys, or schema fields.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Network: yes, but narrowly. The reconciliation adds at most one `GET https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/generation?id=<responseId>` call per OpenRouter chat-completion stream, authenticated with the same API key the request used. There is an 8s timeout and the call fails open so a flaky lookup never breaks a turn. Auth/secrets and tool execution: no change.

What is the highest-risk area?

Latency: the reconciliation adds 50-500ms (per the reporter's own measurement) to the moment the agent observes the `done` event for OpenRouter calls. Because the wrapper mutates the message in place before forwarding, the agent loop sees the reconciled cost but waits for the lookup.

How is that risk mitigated?

The 8s controller-driven timeout caps worst-case latency, the lookup is skipped when the streamed cost is already in parity with the authoritative value (so non-tier-priced models avoid the overhead in practice once the cache pattern lands), and every failure mode (timeout, HTTP error, malformed payload, missing API key, missing responseId, parity result) degrades to a warn log instead of mutating the cost or rejecting the turn. The reconciliation only ever revises cost.total upward; it never lowers it, which matches OpenRouter's tier-priced billing direction.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Await maintainer / ClawSweeper review on (a) whether `usage.cost.total` overwrite is the preferred shape over the alternative \"keep both\" approach the reporter listed as Option 2, and (b) whether the reconciliation should be opt-in per-model.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

A live `qwen/qwen3.6-plus` long-context smoke against the real OpenRouter `/generation` endpoint is still not provided from this environment.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Initial PR; no PR comments yet.

AI-assisted: yes. I reviewed the touched code paths, designed the stream-wrapper boundary, and ran the focused validation commands above.

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

target.title: fix(agents): isolate RTL lines on outbound assistant text
target.body:
## Summary

- Extract the existing TUI-only `applyRtlIsolation` helper into a shared module so the canonical outbound assistant-visible text sanitizer can reuse it.
- Apply U+2067/U+2069 line-level isolation in the `delivery` profile of `sanitizeAssistantVisibleText`, so Hebrew/Arabic/other RTL prose delivered through Discord, WhatsApp, Slack, Telegram, and any other channel plugin keeps trailing LTR punctuation on the visually-correct side.
- Keep the `history` and `internal-scaffolding` profiles byte-for-byte with the model output (transcript storage and runtime inspection stay clean); keep the TUI behavior unchanged.

## Linked context

Closes #68105

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No direct maintainer request; this follows ClawSweeper's triage that flagged the issue as a queueable source-reproducible fix with the existing TUI implementation as the precedent.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Outbound delivery sanitizer drops bidi isolation that the TUI already applies, so Hebrew/Arabic text such as `שלום?` reaches Discord/Slack/Telegram/WhatsApp without RLI/PDI wrappers and renders trailing LTR punctuation on the wrong visual side.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on Linux with Node `v22.22.3`, pnpm `11.2.2` via corepack.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran two local OpenClaw source smokes with `PATH=\"\$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.3/bin:\$PATH\" node --import tsx --input-type=module -e '...'` against the real shared sanitizers, once with the patch temporarily stashed on the same branch and once with it restored.
- Evidence after fix: Copied console output from those local OpenClaw source smokes.

  Before-fix (patch stashed):

  ```
  === BEFORE-FIX BEHAVIOR (RTL bidi missing on outbound) ===
  TUI sanitizeRenderableText  : \"⁧שלום?⁩\" wraps? true
  Outbound sanitizeVisibleText: \"שלום?\" wraps? false
  bug repro confirmed: true
  ```

  After-fix (patch restored):

  ```
  === AFTER-FIX BEHAVIOR ===
  TUI sanitizeRenderableText still wraps: \"⁧שלום?⁩\"
  Outbound delivery now wraps:            \"⁧שלום?⁩\"
  Outbound delivery (Arabic):             \"⁧كيف حالك؟⁩\"
  History profile stays byte-clean:       \"שלום?\"
  Internal-scaffolding stays clean:       \"שלום?\"
  Idempotent (delivery twice === once):   true
  Mixed LTR/RTL outbound:
  \"Hello there.\n⁧مرحبا!⁩\nSee you.\"
  ```

- Observed result after fix: The outbound sanitizer now wraps RTL-script lines line-by-line; LTR-only lines pass through unchanged; mixed-direction text wraps only the RTL lines; the `history` and `internal-scaffolding` profiles intentionally remain byte-for-byte with the model output; the function is idempotent against already-isolated text.
- What was not tested: A live screenshot from Discord/Slack/Telegram showing the visual punctuation move. The fix is at the sanitizer boundary that those channels already call, so the proof above demonstrates the byte-level change at the exact seam those clients consume.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The after-fix proof uses the real OpenClaw shared sanitizers (`sanitizeAssistantVisibleText`, `sanitizeAssistantVisibleTextWithProfile`, `sanitizeRenderableText`) imported from the source checkout, but does not run a full channel send because this environment is not configured with messaging credentials.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Captured above via `git stash`-ing this patch.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `PATH=\"\$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.3/bin:\$PATH\" node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/shared/text/rtl-isolation.test.ts src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.test.ts src/tui/tui-formatters.test.ts`
- `PATH=\"\$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.3/bin:\$PATH\" corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/shared/text/rtl-isolation.ts src/shared/text/rtl-isolation.test.ts src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.ts src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.test.ts src/tui/tui-formatters.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Outputs:

- `rtl-isolation.test.ts` + `assistant-visible-text.test.ts`: `Test Files 2 passed (2)` / `Tests 111 passed (111)` (6 new RTL isolation cases + 5 new delivery-vs-history profile cases land alongside the existing assistant-visible-text and tool-call XML coverage).
- `tui-formatters.test.ts`: `Test Files 1 passed (1)` / `Tests 47 passed (47)` — TUI rendering behavior is unchanged after the helper moved to the shared module.
- `oxfmt --check`: `All matched files use the correct format.`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- New `src/shared/text/rtl-isolation.test.ts` covers Hebrew/Arabic wrapping, LTR passthrough, line-by-line behavior, double-wrap idempotency, and skipping lines that already carry bidi controls.
- New `RTL bidi isolation on outbound delivery` describe block in `src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.test.ts` covers the delivery profile wrap, history and internal-scaffolding profiles staying byte-clean, mixed LTR/RTL line handling, and idempotency at the delivery boundary.

What failed before this fix, if known?

The before-fix smoke above reproduces the missing isolation at the outbound sanitizer boundary; without this patch the new delivery-profile assertions would fail because `sanitizeAssistantVisibleText` returned the raw RTL string.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes (delivery-only). Hebrew, Arabic, and other RTL-script lines now reach messaging surfaces wrapped in U+2067/U+2069, so trailing LTR punctuation visually trails the line as the user expects. LTR-only text and transcript storage are unchanged.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

Bidi control characters leaking into surfaces that do not strip them and then echoing back through history or copy/paste paths.

How is that risk mitigated?

The isolation is applied only to the `delivery` profile, so the `history` profile (transcript storage) and `internal-scaffolding` profile (runtime/UI inspection) continue to receive the raw model output. The line-level guard against existing bidi controls keeps the function idempotent, so messages that re-enter delivery sanitization (e.g. tool result roundtrips) are not re-wrapped.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Await maintainer / ClawSweeper review on whether the chosen canonical boundary (delivery profile of `sanitizeAssistantVisibleText`) is the preferred seam, or whether additional channel-specific sanitizers (for example `sanitizeForPlainText`) should also opt in.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

A live Discord/Slack/Telegram screenshot showing the visual punctuation move is still not provided from this environment.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Initial PR; no PR comments yet.

AI-assisted: yes. I reviewed the touched code paths, extracted the shared helper, and ran the focused validation commands above.

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

target.title: fix(outbound): keep Discord runtime adapters resolvable
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Discord final replies can fail with `Outbound not configured for channel: discord` when the outbound path observes a pinned/setup channel registry that no longer exposes the runtime send/message adapter while the active replacement registry does.
- The bootstrap guard could also become a one-shot miss: if loading returned but did not make the channel send-capable, the same registry/version key suppressed later recovery attempts.

Why does this matter now?

- Issue #90162 reports the final reply delivery failure on v2026.6.1 after the inbound/agent turn had already completed, with restart recovery later succeeding. That matches an outbound registry resolution/retry gap rather than Discord protocol behavior.

What is the intended outcome?

- Outbound resolution treats setup-only shells as non-deliverable when a runtime adapter is required.
- Message/outbound lookup checks the selected channel registry and the distinct active replacement registry, so an active Discord runtime adapter remains resolvable after registry replacement.
- Bootstrap retries when a load attempt does not actually make the channel send-capable.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- Discord API/protocol send behavior and live Discord credential validation.
- Broader plugin loader cache/env-var changes or frontend tool-discovery pinning behavior.

What does success look like?

- Discord final delivery no longer loses the outbound/message adapter solely because the pinned channel registry and active runtime registry diverged.

What should reviewers focus on?

- Whether the runtime-vs-setup-shell distinction is narrow enough for outbound delivery paths.
- Whether checking the active registry fallback from bootstrap/resolution preserves existing read/setup behavior while preventing a setup shell from masking a runtime adapter.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #90162

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #87333 (adjacent channel-registry pinning issue; different files and failure path).

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No maintainer request seen; ClawSweeper marked the issue source-repro/fix-shape-clear.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed:
  Discord final reply delivery could fail with `Outbound not configured for channel: discord` after active registry replacement when the selected channel registry still exposed only a setup shell and the bootstrap attempt guard did not retry after a non-send-capable load.
- Real environment tested:
  Local OpenClaw source worktree `/Users/andy/openclaw-90162-discord-outbound`, branch `fix/90162-discord-outbound-adapter`, using deterministic runtime/source tests. No live Discord credentials are available in this worker.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/outbound/channel-resolution.test.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-bootstrap.runtime.test.ts src/plugins/runtime.channel-pin.test.ts`

  `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts src/infra/outbound/delivery-queue.recovery.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.plugin-dispatch.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.media.test.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-selection.test.ts src/infra/outbound/targets.test.ts`

  `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/message.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-target.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/send.test.ts src/channels/turn/durable-delivery.test.ts`

  `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/infra/outbound/channel-bootstrap.runtime.test.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-bootstrap.runtime.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-resolution.test.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-resolution.ts`

  `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/infra/outbound/channel-bootstrap.runtime.test.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-bootstrap.runtime.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-resolution.test.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-resolution.ts`

  `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`

  `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`

  `git diff --check`

  `codex review --base upstream/main`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text
[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 8.37s
# channel-resolution + channel-bootstrap + runtime.channel-pin: 21 infra tests, 14 plugin tests

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 6.50s
# nearby outbound sweep: 6 files, 252 tests

[test] passed 4 Vitest shards in 17.07s
# gateway/infra/cron/channels consumer sweep: 2 gateway files/112 tests, 1 infra file/11 tests, 1 cron file/53 tests, 1 channels file/6 tests

All matched files use the correct format.

codex review --base upstream/main:
No actionable regressions were identified in the changed outbound channel bootstrap/resolution paths. The added behavior is covered by targeted tests, and nearby outbound/gateway tests passed.
```

- Observed result after fix:
  Source-backed runtime tests now prove that setup-only and actions-only shells do not satisfy runtime outbound resolution, active replacement registries remain visible to message/outbound resolution, and bootstrap retries after a load that still does not make the channel send-capable.
- What was not tested:
  Live Discord final delivery against Discord APIs was not tested.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints:
  No live Discord bot credentials are available in this worker. If live Discord proof is required, a parent-side OpenClaw agent with Discord credentials should capture a final-reply delivery log before/after this source patch.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged):
  The added regression tests model the source-level failure shape from #90162: pinned setup/channel registry plus active runtime replacement and a non-send-capable bootstrap result. These cases would have returned/kept a setup shell or suppressed retry before the resolver/bootstrap changes.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/outbound/channel-resolution.test.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-bootstrap.runtime.test.ts src/plugins/runtime.channel-pin.test.ts` — passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts src/infra/outbound/delivery-queue.recovery.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.plugin-dispatch.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.media.test.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-selection.test.ts src/infra/outbound/targets.test.ts` — passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/message.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-target.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/send.test.ts src/channels/turn/durable-delivery.test.ts` — passed
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/infra/outbound/channel-bootstrap.runtime.test.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-bootstrap.runtime.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-resolution.test.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-resolution.ts` — passed
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/infra/outbound/channel-bootstrap.runtime.test.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-bootstrap.runtime.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-resolution.test.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-resolution.ts` — passed
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo` — passed
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo` — passed
- `git diff --check` — passed
- `codex review --base upstream/main` — no actionable regressions

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- `src/infra/outbound/channel-bootstrap.runtime.test.ts` covers active replacement registry send-capability visibility and retry after a bootstrap attempt that leaves the channel non-send-capable.
- `src/infra/outbound/channel-resolution.test.ts` covers pinned message adapter lookup after active registry replacement, setup shell and actions-only avoidance during bootstrap, active runtime preference over loaded setup shells, and non-return of setup shells after unsuccessful bootstrap.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- A setup-only channel shell could be returned as the outbound plugin for a runtime delivery path, masking a send-capable active runtime plugin or leaving delivery to fail as unsupported.
- Bootstrap could record an attempt even when the channel still could not send, suppressing a later retry in the same registry-version window.

If no test was added, why not?

- Tests were added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Final outbound delivery should now recover through a runtime adapter instead of failing when registry replacement leaves a setup shell in the selected channel registry.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

- Some non-delivery callers might have expected setup-only plugin metadata from `resolveOutboundChannelPlugin({ allowBootstrap: true })`.

How is that risk mitigated?

- The stricter runtime-surface filtering applies to outbound/runtime resolution. Read-only resolver paths still preserve setup/metadata visibility, and targeted plus nearby consumer tests passed.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Ready for maintainer/ClawSweeper review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- CI and ClawSweeper need to run. Live Discord proof is not available from this worker; parent-side credentials/logs can be supplied if ClawSweeper requests them.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- No PR comments yet. The implementation follows the issue hint to prioritize registry/outbound adapter resolution over Discord protocol code.

target.title: feat(voice-call): add Microsoft Teams voice provider (OpenClawTeamsBridge)
target.body:
## Summary

**What problem does this PR solve?**
- OpenClaw's `voice-call` extension supports Twilio, Telnyx, Plivo, and a mock provider, but has no way to handle **Microsoft Teams** calls. Teams audio cannot reach the existing webhook/media-stream plane because Teams calls are delivered through Graph Calling signalling + the `Microsoft.Skype.Bots.Media` AudioSocket, which only run on .NET/Windows.
- This adds a 5th voice-call provider, **`msteams`**, that bridges Teams call audio into the existing voice-call machinery (CallManager, realtime transcription, response generation, telephony TTS).

**Why does this matter now?**
- It unlocks real-time voice agents on Microsoft Teams using the same OpenClaw agent/STT/TTS stack already used for PSTN providers, with no changes to those providers.

**What is the intended outcome?**
- An operator can set `provider: "msteams"` and OpenClaw will accept per-call WebSocket connections from an external Windows worker ("OpenClawTeamsBridge" / AzureBot), authenticate them, and run the full inbound->STT->agent->TTS->outbound loop with barge-in  -  mirroring the streaming Twilio provider but speaking **PCM 16 kHz** instead of mu-law 8 kHz.

**What is intentionally out of scope?**
- The Windows-side worker (Graph Calling notification endpoint + `Skype.Bots.Media` AudioSocket) is a **separate component, not included in this repo**. This PR only adds the OpenClaw-side provider + WebSocket transport it connects to.
- Outbound dialing (initiating Teams calls)  -  the provider is inbound-only and throws on `initiateCall`.

**What does success look like?**
- `provider: "msteams"` binds an HMAC-authenticated WebSocket server; a live Teams call routed through the external worker produces a transcript, an agent reply, and synthesized speech back to the caller, with barge-in.

**What should reviewers focus on?**
- The WebSocket auth/transport in `msteams-media-stream.ts` (HMAC verification, replay window, payload cap, and loopback-by-default bind).
- The msteams config-validation in `config.ts`: required `port` + SecretRef-compatible `sharedSecret`, the `fromNumber` exemption, and `inboundPolicy` defaulting to "open".
- The runtime lifecycle in `runtime.ts`: the media server starts via an awaited `provider.start()` (a failed bind aborts init) and stops on teardown so the port is released.
- That the new provider reuses existing exported helpers (`generateVoiceResponse`, `CallManager.speak`, telephony TTS) rather than duplicating webhook logic.

---

## Linked context

**Which issue does this close?**

Closes # <!-- fill in if a tracking issue exists; otherwise remove -->

**Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?**

Related # <!-- e.g. prior Telnyx/Twilio realtime streaming PRs this mirrors -->

**Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?**

<!-- Note any maintainer/owner request here, or state "Community contribution." -->

---

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `voice-call` had no Microsoft Teams provider  -  `provider: "msteams"` was rejected as unsupported. This PR adds the provider, the HMAC-authenticated WebSocket transport, and the inbound->STT->agent->TTS->outbound pipeline.
- Real environment tested: OpenClaw 2026.6.2 local gateway on Windows Server (Azure VM); `voice-call` provider `msteams`; agent `microsoft-foundry/gpt-5.4`; ElevenLabs realtime STT (`scribe_v2_realtime`, `pcm_16000`) + ElevenLabs TTS; an external Windows worker bridged a real inbound Microsoft Teams call.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. `pnpm build`
  2. `node openclaw.mjs config validate`  -> prints `Config valid`
  3. `node openclaw.mjs gateway run --force`
  4. Placed a real Microsoft Teams call to the bot, spoke several turns out loud, heard the bot's spoken replies, then hung up.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
  (a) Gateway lifecycle log during the call (private identifiers redacted):
  ```
  MsteamsMediaStream listening port=8443 path=/voice/msteams/stream
  [voice-call] msteams realtime transcription provider: elevenlabs
  [voice-call] msteams TTS provider configured
  MsteamsMediaStream: connection open <callId>
  MsteamsProvider: session.start callId=<callId> threadId=<threadId> caller.aadId=<aadId>
  MsteamsMediaStream: connection closed <callId>
  ```
  (b) Agent session trajectory for the same call (`sessionKey: voice:<redacted>`, model `microsoft-foundry/gpt-5.4`)  -  the inbound caller audio was transcribed by ElevenLabs STT and answered by the agent; each `{"spoken":...}` reply is what was synthesized back over the bridge as TTS the caller heard:
  ```
  user  (STT): "How are you, man?"
  asst (spoken): "I'm doing well, man  -  thanks. What can I help you with?"
  user  (STT): "Tell me more about you."
  asst (spoken): "I'm your phone assistant in OpenClaw  -  calm, practical, and here to help with
                  questions, quick research, reminders, messages, and getting things done..."
  user  (STT): "Thank you. And, uh, what is your-"
  asst (spoken): "I'm an AI phone assistant running in OpenClaw. I don't have a body or a personal
                  life, but I can talk with you, look things up, help you plan..."
  user  (STT): "Mm-hmm"
  asst (spoken): "Yeah  -  kind of like a calm digital helper you can just talk to..."
  ```
- Observed result after fix: full bidirectional audio round-trip on a live Teams call  -  caller speech -> ElevenLabs STT transcript -> `gpt-5.4` agent reply -> ElevenLabs TTS played back to the caller, across multiple turns over ~70 s. The speech disfluencies ("uh", "Mm-hmm") and natural turn-taking confirm these are live ASR transcripts (not typed input); the `{"spoken":...}` payloads are exactly what the provider sends to TTS. Session lifecycle (`connection open` / `session.start` / `connection closed`) is verified in the gateway log.
- What was not tested: outbound dialing (the provider is inbound-only and out of scope); sustained barge-in and high-concurrency load have not yet been captured under real calls.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: transcript/reply text is taken from the agent session trajectory because the gateway file log does not record transcript text at info level. Private identifiers (callId, threadId, caller aadId, sessionKey) are redacted.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): before this patch, `provider: "msteams"` produced `Unsupported voice-call provider: msteams`.

### After-fix proof: realtime delegation + recording-status gate (latest commits)

Captured on real inbound Microsoft Teams calls using the realtime (speech-to-speech) path (`realtime.enabled`, provider `openai`, `toolPolicy: "owner"`), with the recording gate at its default (`requireRecordingStatus: true`). Private identifiers redacted; call ids shortened.

- Recording gate refuses, then opens. With no recording signal, asking the assistant to do work was refused out loud ("recording isn't active") and the gateway logged no `recording.status`. Once the external worker reports recording active on call establish, the gateway opens the gate:
  ```
  # delegation refused (no recording signal yet)
  MsteamsProvider: realtime session.start callId=0c005180... caller.aadId=<redacted>

  # next call: worker reports recording active -> gate opens
  MsteamsProvider: realtime session.start callId=2d005480... caller.aadId=<redacted>
  MsteamsProvider: recording.status 2d005480... = active (realtime)
  ```
- Inline consult (`openclaw_agent_consult`). With the gate open, the caller asked a question; the model spoke the "working on it" filler and the OpenClaw agent ran and replied through the realtime model -- confirming `realtime model -> openclaw_agent_consult -> agent -> spoken reply`.
- Background task (`openclaw_agent_task`) -> Teams chat delivery. The caller said "take care of local time in the background and ping me on Teams." The model invoked the task, spoke the ack ("I'll message you on Teams"), the caller hung up, the agent ran the task in the background, and delivered the result to the caller's Teams chat:
  ```
  MsteamsProvider: realtime session.start callId=22005480... caller.aadId=<redacted>
  MsteamsProvider: recording.status 22005480... = active (realtime)        # gate open
  [trace:embedded-run] runId=voice-realtime-task:22005480...               # background agent run (post-ack)
  sent proactive message                                                    # interim "checking now..."
  sent proactive message                                                    # final result to caller's Teams chat
  MsteamsMediaStream: connection closed 22005480...
  ```
  The two messages received in the caller's Teams chat:
  > I'm checking the local machine time now and will send it here shortly.

  > Local machine time: 2026-06-04 05:28:13 +00:00.

  The agent did real work (read the actual machine clock -- `05:28:13`, matching the run time), not a canned reply.
- Pre-recording transcripts are not retained: `recordTranscript` drops turns while the gate is closed, so the consult context only ever contains recording-active text (regression test in `msteams-realtime.test.ts`).

---

## Tests and validation

**Which commands did you run?**
```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
  extensions/voice-call/src/providers/msteams.test.ts \
  extensions/voice-call/src/msteams-media-stream.test.ts
node openclaw.mjs config validate
pnpm build      # clean compile of the changed extension into dist
```
Result:
```
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  18 passed (18)
```
`node openclaw.mjs config validate` -> `Config valid`.

**What regression coverage was added or updated?**
- `extensions/voice-call/src/providers/msteams.test.ts`  -  provider lifecycle: session.start -> CallManager registration, transcript -> response, barge-in (`assistant.cancel`), playTts framing/pacing, hangup/teardown.
- `extensions/voice-call/src/msteams-media-stream.test.ts`  -  WebSocket server: HMAC upgrade verification (accept/reject), replay-window rejection, `session.start`/`session.end`/`audio.frame`/`ping` parsing, outbound `send`/`close`, and **inbound payload-cap rejection** (oversized frame -> close `1009`, added for GHSA-vw3h-q6xq-jjm5).

**What failed before this fix, if known?**
- `provider: "msteams"` was rejected as an unsupported voice-call provider; there was no transport for Teams audio.

**If no test was added, why not?** N/A  -  unit tests added for both the provider and the transport.

---

## Risk checklist

**Did user-visible behavior change?** `Yes (additive, opt-in)`  -  adds a new `msteams` provider option; existing Twilio/Telnyx/Plivo/mock behavior is unchanged. For msteams specifically, `inboundPolicy` now **defaults to `"open"`** (Teams is inbound-first), so msteams calls are accepted unless the operator sets an explicit policy.

**Did config, environment, or migration behavior change?** `Yes`  -  adds `voice-call` config under `provider: "msteams"` (`msteams.{port,bindAddress,path,sharedSecret}`, plus `streaming` and `tts` blocks). `msteams`-scoped config/validation behavior in `config.ts`:
1. `msteams` is exempt from the `fromNumber` requirement (it never places outbound PSTN calls).
2. `inboundPolicy` defaults to `"open"` for `msteams` when unset.
3. **Required transport:** when `provider: "msteams"`, `validateProviderConfig` now **requires** `msteams.port` and `msteams.sharedSecret` (so a misconfigured runtime fails validation instead of starting but never binding). `msteams.path` has a schema default (`/voice/msteams/stream`).
4. **SecretRef-compatible secret:** `msteams.sharedSecret` accepts the same `SecretInput` shape as `twilio.authToken` (string or `${ENV}`/secret ref), resolved via `resolveMsteamsSharedSecret` and registered in `configContracts.secretInputs`.
No data migration.

**Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change?** `Yes`  -  introduces a **WebSocket server** (operator-configured port) that accepts connections from the external worker.

**What is the highest-risk area?** The WebSocket transport (`msteams-media-stream.ts`)  -  it listens on a network port and accepts external connections carrying call audio.

**How is that risk mitigated?**
- **Loopback by default:** the WS server binds `127.0.0.1` unless the operator sets `msteams.bindAddress` to a specific trusted-network interface  -  it is never exposed on all interfaces implicitly.
- Every HTTP upgrade is authenticated with **HMAC-SHA256** over `timestamp + callId` using a shared secret, compared in constant time (`safeEqualSecret`).
- **Replay protection:** upgrades whose timestamp drifts beyond a configurable window (default 60 s) are rejected.
- **DoS bounds:** 64 KB inbound payload cap; caps on total / pending / per-IP connections.
- Inbound message bodies are validated with a strict `zod` discriminated union.
- **SecretRef secret:** the shared secret is a `SecretInput` (supports `${ENV}`/secret refs), required by config validation, and never logged.
- **Lifecycle-managed:** the server is started as an awaited step of runtime init (a failed bind aborts startup and is cleaned up, rather than being swallowed) and stopped on runtime teardown (the port is released; no orphaned listener).

---

## Current review state

**What is the next action?**
- Maintainer decision on the **product boundary** (see below) and review of the WebSocket auth/transport.

**What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?**
- Maintainer guidance on whether `msteams` ships bundled in `voice-call` or as a separate optional plugin (see Product boundary).

**Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?**
- **Bind-address control (P1):** the WS listener now binds loopback by default and exposes `msteams.bindAddress`; it no longer implicitly binds all interfaces.
- **Required transport + SecretRef secret (P1):** `provider: "msteams"` now fails validation without `msteams.port` + `msteams.sharedSecret`; the secret is `SecretInput`/`SecretRef`-compatible and registered in `configContracts.secretInputs` (so it no longer starts a runtime that cannot accept calls safely).
- **Lifecycle (P1):** the WS server start is an awaited runtime-init step (failed binds surface and are cleaned up) and is stopped on teardown (no orphaned port). Moved out of the provider constructor's fire-and-forget start.
- **Live audio round-trip (P1):** end-to-end caller->STT->agent->TTS->caller was captured on a real multi-turn Teams call (see Real behavior proof).
- **GitHub Advanced Security (GHSA-vw3h-q6xq-jjm5):** 64 KB `maxPayload` on inbound frames; regression test (oversized frame -> close `1009`).
- Internal worker/product names and phased-task references were removed from comments for upstream.

---

## Product boundary (maintainer decision)

`VISION.md` sets a high bar for bundling optional/commercial integrations, while the docs state `voice-call` owns call transport. This PR is written to be **additive and opt-in** and to keep that decision easy either way:
- It adds a 5th provider behind `provider: "msteams"`; default behavior of Twilio/Telnyx/Plivo/mock is unchanged, and nothing activates unless an operator configures it.
- It introduces **no new runtime dependencies** (reuses `ws`, `zod`, and existing plugin-sdk helpers) and **no commercial SDK**  -  Teams-specific Graph/`Skype.Bots.Media` code lives entirely in the out-of-repo Windows worker.
- msteams-specific logic is isolated (`providers/msteams.ts`, `msteams-media-stream.ts`, `msteams-tts.ts`, `msteams.runtime.ts`) with only thin, guarded hooks in shared files, so it can be lifted into a **separate optional plugin** if maintainers prefer that over bundling.

Author is happy to follow maintainer preference on bundled-vs-external packaging.

---

## Files changed (feature scope)

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `extensions/voice-call/src/providers/msteams.ts` | New `msteams` provider: explicit awaited `start()` (binds the WS server; replaces constructor fire-and-forget), inbound audio -> STT, response generation, TTS playback with paced PCM framing, barge-in, CallManager integration, `stop()` for teardown. |
| `extensions/voice-call/src/providers/msteams.test.ts` | Provider unit tests (updated to `await provider.start()`; connect over `127.0.0.1`). |
| `extensions/voice-call/src/msteams-media-stream.ts` | HMAC-authenticated WebSocket server; **binds loopback by default with optional `bindAddress`**; session.start/end/audio.frame/ping protocol; 64 KB payload cap; outbound send/close. |
| `extensions/voice-call/src/msteams-media-stream.test.ts` | Transport unit tests (connect over `127.0.0.1`). |
| `extensions/voice-call/src/msteams-tts.ts` | Teams PCM TTS helper. |
| `extensions/voice-call/src/msteams.runtime.ts` | Resolves the realtime-transcription provider + telephony TTS and injects CallManager/response runtime for msteams. |
| `extensions/voice-call/src/runtime.ts` | Resolves msteams provider with `bindAddress` + resolved `sharedSecret`; **starts the WS server as an awaited runtime-init step and registers `provider.stop()` in the resource lifecycle** (bind failures surface; port released on teardown). |
| `extensions/voice-call/src/config.ts` | `MsteamsConfigSchema` (`port`/`bindAddress`/`path`(default)/`sharedSecret` as `SecretInput`); `resolveMsteamsSharedSecret`; msteams exempt from `fromNumber`; `inboundPolicy` defaults to `"open"`; **requires `msteams.port` + `msteams.sharedSecret`**. |
| `extensions/voice-call/openclaw.plugin.json` | configSchema adds `msteams.bindAddress` and `sharedSecret` (`string`/object); registers `msteams.sharedSecret` in `configContracts.secretInputs`. |

target.title: Enable native meta skill orchestration
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds a native meta skill foundation in OpenClaw: typed frontmatter parsing, catalog projection, DAG runner, template rendering, pause/resume primitives, SQLite run-state schema, gates, auto-propose helpers, and a bundled `meta-skill-creator` flow.
- Wires the runnable default subset into the agent tool surface through `meta_invoke`: `user_input` plans can pause/resume from supplied input, and lifecycle-governed `tool_call` plans can execute only through the final post-policy tool surface.
- Keeps generated skill writes behind the existing Skill Workshop proposal surface: `meta-skill-creator` now calls `skill_workshop` directly, and the duplicate model-facing `meta_skill_creator` tool has been removed.
- Updates shrinkwrap generation so repair logic respects concrete dependency edges without rewriting packages for peer dependency ranges.

## Scope boundary

This draft does not claim full external executor parity yet.

- Default runtime exposed by this PR: `user_input`, plus `tool_call` when the active agent runtime supplies the lifecycle executor and final target-tool ref.
- Parsed/stored groundwork exists for broader step kinds (`llm_chat`, `llm_classify`, `agent`, `skill_exec`), but they are not exposed by the default runtime unless a caller supplies an explicit runner.
- Default runtime hides unsupported plans and multi-step `user_input` plans that would require persisted resume routing.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

None.

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related to the native meta skill migration work.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Draft PR for early visibility; author will refresh any requested proof before marking ready.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: Default runnable meta skills can be cataloged and invoked through `meta_invoke`; generated skill creation remains governed by Skill Workshop; unsupported executor families are not exposed by the default runtime.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on `feature/meta-skill-migration`, Node 22.19.0, pnpm 11.2.2.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node --import tsx <meta state fresh-install/additive-upgrade smoke>`
  - `CI=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=600000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/skills/meta src/agents/meta-invoke-runtime.test.ts src/agents/tools/meta-invoke-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/skill-workshop-tool.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.meta-invoke.test.ts src/agents/tool-catalog.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt-tool-construction-plan.test.ts src/skills/loading/workspace-snapshot.test.ts src/state/openclaw-state-db.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
  - `CI=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=600000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/generate-npm-shrinkwrap.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
  - `corepack pnpm build`
  - `git diff --check openclaw/main...HEAD`
  - Privacy/local-path/secret-shape scan over `openclaw/main...HEAD` diff and commit metadata.
  - Dependency audit for Socket warning scope.
- Evidence after fix:
  - Meta/runtime/state focused suite: 3 Vitest shards passed; 10 files / 76 tests.
  - Shrinkwrap generator suite: 1 Vitest shard passed; 1 file / 26 tests.
  - Build passed: `tsdown`, CLI bootstrap import guard, runtime postbuild, plugin SDK dts, plugin SDK export check, bundled assets, and Control UI production build.
  - SQLite smoke created and read back `meta_skill_runs`, `meta_skill_steps`, `meta_skill_pauses`, and `meta_skill_evidence` on a fresh DB; additive upgrade also preserved an existing `auth_profile_stores` row while adding the meta tables.
  - Privacy scan found no local paths, personal email, source-project names, common token/private-key shapes, or agent-process residue in the PR diff; commit author and committer are both `nice-code-la <nice-code-la@users.noreply.github.com>`.
  - Socket's WhatsApp `baileys` / `whatsapp-rust-bridge` warning is not introduced by this PR: `extensions/whatsapp/npm-shrinkwrap.json` has no diff in this branch, and the same entries exist on `openclaw/main`.
- Observed result after fix: `meta_invoke` is materialized only with a meta catalog and runner; nested `tool_call` execution is constrained to the final safe tool surface; the legacy `meta_skill_creator` tool is not registered; the bundled `meta-skill-creator` flow routes generated skill content through `skill_workshop`.
- What was not tested: full remote Crabbox/Testbox proof; default runtime execution for `llm_chat`, `llm_classify`, `agent`, and `skill_exec` because those executor families are intentionally not exposed without an explicit runner.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this remains a Draft PR. Broad changed-lane calculation in this checkout is misleading because `origin/main` points at the fork; PR-range proof uses `openclaw/main...HEAD`.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node --import tsx <meta state fresh-install/additive-upgrade smoke>`: passed.
- Focused meta/runtime/state suite: 3 Vitest shards passed; 10 files / 76 tests.
- Shrinkwrap generator suite: 1 Vitest shard passed; 1 file / 26 tests.
- `corepack pnpm build`: passed.
- `git diff --check openclaw/main...HEAD`: passed.
- Privacy/local-path/secret-shape scan over the PR diff and commit metadata: clean.
- Dependency audit for the Socket WhatsApp warning: confirmed not introduced by this PR.

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Meta parser, catalog, template, runner, pause/resume, runtime, gates, auto-propose, store, and agent `meta_invoke` runtime tests.
- Tool catalog and construction-plan tests cover `meta_invoke` without a duplicate `meta_skill_creator` tool.
- Skill Workshop tests cover same-name pending proposal revision, exact-match create behavior, and create-to-update routing for existing writable workspace skills.
- Workspace snapshot tests ensure hidden skills are not exposed through the meta catalog.
- Shrinkwrap generator regression coverage for concrete dependency repair without peer dependency rewrites.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- ClawSweeper identified the duplicate `meta_skill_creator` proposal tool as overlapping the existing `skill_workshop` contract; this branch now keeps one proposal tool.
- A first local autoreview pass found two regressions while removing the bridge: fuzzy pending-proposal matching on create, and lost update-proposal behavior for existing writable skills. Both are fixed and covered by tests.

If no test was added, why not?

Tests were added or updated for the changed runtime and generator behavior.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. The state schema gains meta skill runtime tables.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. This adds a model-facing meta invocation tool and governs nested tool execution through existing lifecycle policy.

What is the highest-risk area?

- Tool execution and visibility policy around `meta_invoke`.
- Default pause/resume semantics for meta plans.
- Generated shrinkwrap repair behavior.

How is that risk mitigated?

- `meta_invoke` nested tool calls use the lifecycle executor and final target tool ref.
- Recursive/meta wrapper targets are blocked in the agent meta runtime.
- Hidden skills are excluded from the meta invocation catalog.
- Default runtime does not expose unsupported executor families or multi-turn `user_input` plans until the runtime owns those paths.
- Skill proposal creation stays behind `skill_workshop`, including proposal lifecycle gates.
- Shrinkwrap repair only treats concrete dependencies and optional dependencies as installed edges.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Keep as Draft PR while ClawSweeper and maintainers review the refreshed branch.
- Address CI/reviewer feedback and add any requested remote proof before marking ready.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- Waiting on CI and refreshed ClawSweeper review.
- Remote Crabbox/Testbox proof has not been run in this pass.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- ClawSweeper feedback to keep a single Skill Workshop proposal tool was addressed.
- Local autoreview findings were fixed and the final rerun was clean.
